title: FACTBOX: Aluminum prices surge as Middle East war disrupts GCC trade flows
summary: The war in the Middle East has disrupted aluminum production and exports from Gulf Cooperation Council countries, which produced 6.16 million metric tons in 2025, about 8% of global output. Key smelters like Emirates Global Aluminium, Alba, and others are affected. US Midwest aluminum premiums have hit records due to these disruptions and tariffs.

title: Aluminium prices expected to rise with prolonged war set to hit Gulf producers
summary: Aluminum prices are expected to stay elevated due to ongoing war disruptions, with LME prices reaching $3,544/tonne amid Strait of Hormuz shipping closures. Bahrain's Alba declared force majeure, and Qatar’s Qatalum shut down production due to gas shortages. Analysts warn of potential prices toward $4,000/tonne if disruptions persist.

title: The Silver Squeeze: Aluminum Surges Past $3000 as 2026 Supply Crunch Hits Global Markets
summary: Aluminum prices breached $3,000/tonne on the LME due to supply deficits from China's production caps and demand from energy transition sectors. Geopolitical chokepoints exacerbate the crunch, turning China into a net importer. Producers like Rio Tinto and Norsk Hydro benefit from high prices.

title: War Disruptions Trigger Smelting Capacity Crisis
summary: Middle East war has caused aluminum prices to surge past $3,500/tonne, with shuttered smelting capacity and high electricity costs due to shipping lane closures. Emirates Global Aluminium faces export blockades despite ongoing operations. US producers like Century Aluminum are restarting plants to fill the gap.

title: Prysmian Partners with Rio Tinto for Innovative Aluminum Solution with ELYSIS™ Accelerating Sustainability Commitment
summary: Prysmian has partnered with Rio Tinto to produce building wire using ELYSIS™ aluminum, which eliminates direct greenhouse gas emissions from smelting and emits oxygen instead. This innovation supports lower carbon emissions in wire and cable production and aligns with sustainability goals for data centers and net zero targets by 2035. The partnership builds on a 2023 supply agreement for low-carbon aluminum from renewable hydropower.

title: ALUMINIUM 2026 Launches New Industry Innovation Awards
summary: ALUMINIUM 2026 introduces Innovation Awards celebrating breakthroughs in sustainable manufacturing, technology, and smart automation. Categories include Sustainable & Low-Carbon Manufacturing, Technology & Product Innovation, and Smart Manufacturing, Automation & Connectivity. Entries from exhibitors open in May, with industry voting in September on innovations from the past 48 months.

title: IBAAS–2026 to Spotlight Innovation, Sustainability and Digital Transformation in the Global Aluminium Industry
summary: IBAAS 2026 will unite global aluminum experts in India, focusing on bauxite innovation, recycling, green aluminum, AI, and sustainable practices. Key themes include bauxite beneficiation, red mud management, value recovery from wastes, advanced alloys, cathode technologies, circular economy, and Industry 4.0 digitalization. The event drives transformation toward low-carbon and resource-efficient production.

title: Aluminum at the Centre of Global Industrial Transformation AICE 2026
summary: AICE 2026 in Suzhou gathers the global aluminum industry to address sustainability, standards, and innovation across the value chain for infrastructure and manufacturing. Regulatory pressures and commitments accelerate investments in scrap collection, sorting, and remelting technologies. The event highlights aluminum's role in lighter materials and the shift to sustainable production.

title: Why Is Aluminum Scrap Suddenly Shaping the Future? The 2026 Global Shockwave Explained
summary: The aluminum scrap market is booming due to policy shifts, rising prices, and demand for sustainability, with innovations in closed-loop recycling, green smelting, and sorting systems. Recycled aluminum uses 95% less energy than primary production, aiding carbon reduction and circular economy goals. Regions like Asia-Pacific see surging demand from construction, automotive, and electronics sectors.

title: Recycled AL & Sustainability update: Plant fire, low-carbon cable trials, recycling gains and major global expansions
summary: Recent developments in recycled aluminum and sustainability include plant fire disruptions, low-carbon cable trials, recycling advancements, and strategic global expansions. These updates shape the industry's push toward lower emissions and efficient practices. The news highlights ongoing innovations amid challenges.

title: Global Iron and Steel Prices and News 26.03.2026
summary: India's flat steel market remains firm, with HRC prices holding near multi-month highs amid strong demand and restocking activity. Russia's potential excise duty on imported steel and EU revisions to the Carbon Border Adjustment Mechanism are highlighted. U.S. steel imports rose 0.8% monthly in February, while Ukraine’s scrap export ban pressures the market.

title: Steel - Price - Chart - Historical Data - News
summary: Steel prices fell to 3,122 CNY/T on March 26, 2026, down 0.29% from the previous day. Steel rebar futures dropped to around CNY 3,130 per ton due to Tangshan's level-two emergency response amid heavy air pollution, raising concerns over output curbs. Chinese steel production declined 3.6% in February, with global output falling 2.2%.

title: Steel Market Forecast 2026-2027 | Global Price Outlook & Analysis
summary: Major regulatory developments are reshaping steel trade flows and prices in 2026–27, particularly in Europe, with ferrous scrap prices rangebound in the $350–380/t range. Little meaningful steel price recovery is expected globally in 2026 due to overcapacity, weak Chinese demand, and declining raw materials, with upturns more likely in 2027.

title: Steel Imports Up 0.8% In February vs. January
summary: The U.S. imported 1,663,000 net tons of steel in February 2026, up 0.8% from January, while finished steel imports fell 6.2%. Year-to-date imports are down significantly compared to 2025, with finished steel import market share at 15%. Over the 12-month period to February 2026, total steel imports declined 20.3%.

title: U.S. Steel Manufacturers Plan Near-Term Capacity Additions
summary: U.S. steel manufacturers are planning over $6 billion in capital projects starting in Q2 2026 to boost capacity and efficiency. United States Steel is adding a premium thread line in Alabama and upgrading its Mon Valley Works in Pennsylvania. These investments focus on operational improvements and reducing pollution amid shifts to EAF technology.

title: Copper Supply Crunch Intensifies: Impact on Global Renewable Energy Sector
summary: Global copper markets face a structural supply deficit with prices on the London Metal Exchange ranging from $12,000 to $14,500 per metric ton. The crisis stems from chronic underinvestment in new mines combined with major operational disruptions in 2025, including a mudslide at Indonesia's Grasberg mine that triggered a force majeure. China's top copper smelters cut production by over 10% for 2026 due to collapsed treatment and refining charges.

title: Chile's copper output, manufacturing production decline in January
summary: Chile, the world's largest copper producer, saw copper output fall 3% year-on-year in January 2026 to 413,712 metric tons. Manufacturing production in the country also declined 3.8% on a yearly basis during the same period.

title: How Will Tight Copper Market Affect Data Center Growth?
summary: A looming copper shortage threatens data center expansion plans worth $600+ billion in 2026, as the International Copper Study Group predicts a 150,000 metric-ton deficit. The U.S. launched Project Vault with $10 billion in EXIM loans and $2 billion in private investment to create a Strategic Critical Minerals Reserve, while major projects like the Vicuna copper project in South America could eventually produce over 500,000 metric tons annually.

title: Copper Weekly Brief - 27th February 2026
summary: Copper traded around $13,000 per ton in late February, remaining strong year-on-year despite slight easing from recent records. Exchange inventories reached 20-year highs, but analysts attribute this to timing and logistics rather than true surplus, with forecasters expecting 2026 to be a consolidation year in a continuing bull market driven by electrification and grid investment.

title: Barrick Mining delays copper-gold project
summary: Barrick Mining is delaying its major copper and gold project in Pakistan due to Middle East conflict and security concerns in the region.

title: Copper Supply Crunch Intensifies: Impact on Global Renewable Energy Sector
summary: A global copper supply deficit is hindering the transition to carbon-neutral energy, with prices soaring to $12,000-$14,500 per ton due to demand from EVs and power grids. Short-term innovations include copper substitution with aluminum and rare-earth-free motors, while long-term solutions focus on recycling, urban mining, and potential deep-sea mining. Investors should note leverage for major miners like BHP and FCX amid rising costs for renewables.

title: Economic Survey 2026: World faces copper shortage as AI data centres drive power demand
summary: The Economic Survey warns of an impending global copper shortage driven by surging power demand from AI data centers, turning critical minerals into strategic bottlenecks. Copper prices are volatile due to mine outages in key regions, with massive ore processing required for production, such as 167-200 tons per ton of copper. Trade restrictions exacerbate supply deficits amid growing demand from power sectors worldwide.

title: Ten major mining tech trends in 2026: Part 1
summary: Innovations in copper mining include reprocessing waste from major mines like El Teniente and Freeport-McMoRan sites to produce significant copper volumes without new mines. Freeport aims for 360,000 tons per annum from sulphide waste using advanced leaching, while Atlantic Copper plans to extract multiple metals from e-waste. Technologies also target up to 78% reduction in water use per ton of copper through innovative flowsheets.

title: Copper To Become the Global Economy's Most Critical Asset in 2026
summary: Global copper markets face deficits of hundreds of thousands of tons in 2026 due to underinvestment in mines and surging demand from AI data centers, cloud infrastructure, and electrification. Data centers require massive copper for wiring, power systems, and cooling, creating price-inelastic demand amid supply constraints. This shift positions copper as a scarcity premium asset with new consumption categories rivaling traditional uses.

title: Nickel prices rose 1.01%, and asphalt surged nearly 50% year-to-date
summary: Nickel prices rose 1.01% during the trading session on March 27. Asphalt prices surged nearly 50% year-to-date. This reflects positive movements in the commodities market.

title: Metals market on March 27: Nickel and tin prices recover, asphalt prices rise strongly
summary: On March 27, 2026, nickel prices rose 1.01% to $17,338 per ton, with a weekly increase of 2.65%. This recovery reflects positive expectations in battery and renewable energy sectors. Base metals like aluminum and zinc also saw gains.

title: Middle East war pressures copper prices, could impact nickel production
summary: The article examines how Middle East geopolitical tensions and Strait of Hormuz closure are affecting global commodity markets, with particular focus on nickel production. It highlights that PT Weda Bay Nickel's production quota was cut to 12 million wet metric tons for 2026 from 42 million wmt in 2025, and discusses how sulfur supply chain disruptions threaten HPAL operations critical for battery-grade nickel production.

title: AKP to maintain 2026 nickel output after strong 2025 growth, continues infrastructure build-out
summary: PT Adhi Kartiko Pratama Tbk (AKP), a nickel miner, plans to maintain its 2026 nickel output following strong growth in 2025 while continuing infrastructure development.

title: Metals market on March 27: Nickel and tin prices recover, asphalt prices surge
summary: Global metals markets showed positive developments with nickel prices rising 1.01% to $17,338 per ton and gaining 2.65% on a weekly basis, reflecting positive expectations in battery and renewable energy sectors.

title: Can nickel prices hit $25000 in 2026?
summary: The article analyzes Indonesia's strategic shift from maximizing supply to maximizing value through supply cuts and quota reductions for 2026. It notes that only 55% of Indonesia's approved nickel ore production capacity was utilized in 2025, and discusses how the country is implementing stricter environmental enforcement and banning new NPI smelters.

title: INTERVIEW: Nickel Industries to add battery products, grow ore throughput fivefold by 2026
summary: Nickel Industries plans to expand its product mix to include nickel cathode and nickel sulfate while increasing ore extraction to 25 million mt in 2026 from 19 million mt in 2024. The company is commissioning a second mine (Sampala) expected to enter production within 12 months.
